codeReview.summary: All 14 palette colors at commit time correctly have R=G=B (pure grey). Target values from the feature description are met exactly for explicitly specified constants (BG_BASE, BG_CONTENT, CHROME_BG, CHROME_BORDER, TEXT_PRIMARY, TEXT_CURSOR, TEXT_SELECTION, SHADOW_PEAK). SHADOW_DEPTH updated from 8 to 12 in compositor. Comments updated to reflect monochrome theme. Visual verification was thorough — pixel-level inspection confirmed pure grey tones, chrome translucency (alpha blending visible), shadow gradient across 12px depth, and bright white cursor. Clean build, all 869 tests pass.

issues.description: CHROME_BG comment dropped 'status bar' reference ('title bar, status bar' → 'title bar') even though the status bar still existed at commit time. Moot now — resolved by subsequent commit f5bdfd0 which removed the status bar entirely.

Feature description says 'chrome text ~RGB(200+)' but CHROME_SUBTITLE=100 and CHROME_STATUS=140 are well below 200. This is reasonable design hierarchy (secondary/tertiary text intentionally dimmer), and the '~' prefix suggests a guideline not a hard requirement. CHROME_TITLE=210 and CHROME_CLOCK=200 meet the target for primary chrome text.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'When changing a constant value, search for ALL comments and docstrings referencing the old value and update them.' The worker updated comments for changed constants but also proactively narrowed the CHROME_BG comment scope (removing 'status bar') beyond what was required for the value change. This was ultimately correct (status bar was removed in the next commit) but at commit time was technically inaccurate.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Diff shows CHROME_BG comment changed from 'title bar, status bar' to 'title bar' at commit 3163ec9, but status bar wasn't removed until f5bdfd0.

summary: Clean pass. All palette colors are pure monochrome grey (R=G=B verified in both source and pixel-level QEMU screenshot inspection). SHADOW_DEPTH correctly increased to 12px. Chrome translucency, shadow gradient, cursor brightness, and text readability all verified visually. Two non-blocking issues: a slightly premature comment edit (resolved by next commit) and secondary chrome text being intentionally dimmer than the '200+' guideline (reasonable design hierarchy). Worker followed the os-worker skill procedure precisely with exemplary visual verification.
